results.title: How to put a condom on | NHS
results.reason: Produced by the NHS, this provides the most essential, clear, and accurate practical skill for any beginner in a concise format.
summary: Check the expiration date and package air
Squeeze the tip to remove trapped air
Roll the condom down to the base
Take it off carefully after you finish

results.title: First Time Sex Checklist #ThatSexEdTalk
results.reason: Features a medical professional addressing common first-time anxieties, making it highly relevant for the beginner level.
summary: Make sure both people agree and feel ready
Talk about protection and health safety first
Choose a private place where you feel safe
Focus on comfort rather than just the act

results.title: Why We Need Intimacy (More Than Sex)
results.reason: Excellent for beginners to understand the emotional and psychological components of sex beyond just the physical act.
summary: Learn how emotional closeness builds strong bonds
Understand why feeling seen and heard matters
Discover different ways to show you care
Build trust through honest and open sharing

results.title: Anal Sex Prep
results.reason: The channel is highly regarded for clinical yet accessible sex education, though this specific topic is a niche sub-set of the beginner level.
summary: Use plenty of lubricant for better comfort
Go very slowly and listen to your body
Clean the area gently before you start
Communicate clearly with your partner throughout

results.title: Watch this before your first time | ft. Dr. Tanushree Panday
results.reason: Similar to video 3, it uses expert testimony to debunk myths and provide a structured 'checklist' for beginners.
summary: Understand that pain is not always expected
Learn why staying relaxed helps the body
Get facts about how your body works
Focus on feeling safe with your partner
